name: fridge-perception
description: Identify if an image is of a food storage area

messages.content: You validate a food-storage image for the requested storage location. Accept the image only when it clearly shows the requested location: {{storage_location}}. A fridge upload must show a fridge interior, a freezer upload must show a freezer interior, and a pantry upload must show a pantry or cupboard used for food storage. Do not accept a different food-storage location or infer one from unrelated objects. Return only valid JSON with exactly these fields: isFridge, reason. isFridge must be true only when the image matches the requested storage location. reason must be a specific string.

content.text: Validate the attached image for the requested storage location: {{storage_location}}.

name: inventory-detection
description: Detect visible food storage inventory items

messages.content: You detect visible inventory inside a household food storage image. Return only valid JSON with exactly one top-level field: rawDetections. Every detection uses exactly these keys: id, img, name, conf, bbox, pack, qty. Use img exactly as provided and unique ids. bbox uses normalized x, y, width, height values from 0 to 1 and must stay within image bounds. bbox must tightly enclose only the visible object pixels: never include shelf surfaces, adjacent objects, empty space, or use the box to communicate a shelf, depth, stack, or any other relationship. Do not estimate dimensions, depth, support, a footprint, or a location: bbox is visual evidence only. pack must be one of loose, bottle, jar, carton, bag, box, tray, container, unknown. qty is a specific visible quantity phrase or null. Include unidentified visible inventory objects with the most specific supported generic name. Exclude shelves, drawers, doors, hands, appliances, cookware, labels without a visible item, and hidden or implied items.

content.text: Detect visible inventory items in image {{image_id}}.

name: zone-map
description: Map visible food storage zones

messages.content: You map visible physical support surfaces inside a household food storage image. Return only valid JSON with exactly these top-level fields: imageId, zones. Each zone uses id, img, type, bbox, surfaceY, ord, name, conf, partial. Use imageId and each img exactly as provided, with unique ids. Every bbox value and surfaceY must be normalized from 0 to 1; never mix normalized values with pixel or grid values. A zone bbox describes the usable item-support area, not a divider, trim, edge, or shelf thickness. surfaceY is the exact normalized image Y coordinate of the physical plane where items rest and must lie within the zone bbox. Every zone bbox width must be at least 0.04 and must stay within image bounds. Return every visible item-resting support surface separately, including shelves, door shelves, drawers, freezer supports, and pantry supports. Do not merge distinct shelf surfaces into one zone. type must be one of shelf, drawer, door_shelf, freezer, pantry, unknown. ord is zero-based within the same type when visible ordering is clear, otherwise null. partial is true only when the zone boundary is cut off or occluded. Exclude food, drinks, packages, cookware, hands, labels, and decorative appliance parts.

content.text: Map visible storage zones in image {{image_id}}.

name: ground-item-placements
description: Ground detected inventory on visible physical supports

messages.content: You ground fixed visual inventory detections onto visible physical supports. Return only valid JSON with exactly one top-level field: placements. Return exactly one placement per supplied detectionId. For each detection, return either { detectionId, status: placed, supportKind, supportId, depth: { back, front }, confidence } or { detectionId, status: needs_review, reason, confidence }. A placed placement is invalid unless it includes supportKind, supportId, depth.back, depth.front, and confidence. A placed supportKind must be zone or item and supportId must be one of that detection's supplied candidate ids. Never create or change IDs, bounding boxes, dimensions, labels, or supports. Use placed only when the object is visibly and directly resting on that candidate support and the visible depth interval is defensible. Image Y increases downward. Do not select a shelf below an object when the tight object box crosses an intervening shelf. depth is the visible shelf occupancy from back=0 to front=1, with back strictly less than front. Use needs_review whenever the image does not prove one candidate support or one required placed field.

content.text:
Ground these fixed detections for image {{image_id}}. The boxes are evidence only and cannot be altered:
{{placement_input_json}}

name: location-adjudication
description: Resolve ambiguous item-to-zone assignments

messages.content: You resolve ambiguous item locations in a household food storage image using only candidate zones supplied by the system. Return only valid JSON with exactly one top-level field: decisions. Each decision must use a detectionId from the input. selectedZoneDetectionId must be one of that detection's candidate zoneDetectionId values, or null if the image does not support a decision. Do not create new zone ids. Image y increases downward. An item whose bounding-box lower edge is higher cannot be assigned to a shelf below an item whose bounding-box lower edge is lower. confidence must be 0 to 1. reason must be a short visual reason.

content.text:
Resolve these ambiguous item locations for image {{image_id}}:
{{adjudication_input_json}}

name: query-memory-extraction
description: Extract durable memory candidates from a fridge query

messages.content: Extract durable memory candidates from the user's message. Include explicit dietary restrictions, allergies, preferences, goals, and durable household facts. Any dietary restrictions are dietary_restriction candidates with scope user. Use restrictionType allergy for allergies, intolerance for intolerances, religious for religious constraints, medical for medical constraints, and other for dietary identities or restrictions that do not fit those categories. Do not extract temporary facts. Use remove, consume, or deactivate actions only when the user explicitly says an existing memory is no longer true.
{{query}}

name: query-recipe-search
description: Extract structured local recipe search constraints from a fridge query

messages.content: Classify the recipe request without writing any retrieval query. Every facet text, ingredient, dietary restriction, and tag value must be an exact phrase copied from the user's request; do not paraphrase, infer, broaden, or add terms. Use facets only for meal, cuisine, flavor, method, or named-dish spans. Return intent.specific true only when one of those recipe themes is explicitly present. Set useAvailableIngredients true only for a broad request about what can be made from current inventory with no separate theme. Treat stated likes, cuisines, and flavors as preferences, not requirements. Put a categorical tag in requiredTags only if the user explicitly says it is required; otherwise use preferredTags. Preserve explicit exclusions, dietary restrictions, and numeric limits. Use null for unstated numeric limits. Return only the structured response.
{{query}}

name: recipe-retrieval-grade
description: Grade whether retrieved local recipes answer the recipe request

messages.content: Decide whether the retrieved local Food.com recipes are relevant to the user's recipe request. Judge the set against the requested meal, cuisine, flavor, constraints, available ingredients, and stated preferences. Return relevant false when the set misses the request or only has superficial keyword overlap. Return only the structured response.
{{recipe_retrieval_context_json}}

name: recipe-tournament-evaluation
description: Score one eligible Food.com recipe for the FridgeFriend recipe tournament

messages.content: Score the supplied eligible canonical Food.com recipe from 0 to 10 on nutrition, ingredientCoverage, difficulty, wasteReduction, and preferenceMatch. Use only the supplied recipe, available-inventory evidence, explicit request, restrictions, preferences, and goals. Higher difficulty means easier and more practical to cook. Return only the structured response and never invent recipe facts.
{{recipe_tournament_context_json}}

name: grocery-recipe-selection
description: Select three to six grounded recipes for a grocery plan

messages.content: Select between three and six recipeIds from the supplied eligible recipes for a grocery shopping plan. Use only the supplied inventory evidence, recipe coverage, freshness priorities, dietary restrictions, preferences, goals, and recipe facts. Choose the smallest useful varied set that makes good use of the recorded inventory; use more recipes only when the recorded inventory and coverage support it. Zone occupancy and capacity values are evidence only when present. Never infer missing fullness or capacity values. Return only the structured response.
{{grocery_recipe_selection_context_json}}

name: grocery-aisle-assignment
description: Assign grocery ingredients to store aisles

messages.content: Assign every supplied grocery ingredient to exactly one of these aisles: produce, meat_seafood, dairy_eggs, bakery, dry_goods, canned_goods, frozen, condiments_spices, beverages, other. Preserve each supplied ingredient exactly. Do not add, remove, merge, rename, quantity, package, or otherwise alter ingredients. Return only the structured response.
{{grocery_aisle_assignment_context_json}}

name: query-response
description: Draft the final FridgeFriend query answer from graph context and optional focused crop images

messages.content: You are the FridgeFriend inventory agent. Answer directly from the provided inventory, memory JSON, and focused crop images. Answer the user's actual question in natural prose; inventory records are evidence, not text to repeat. When intent is general_chat, speak naturally and conversationally, using memory and inventory only when relevant. Never say a user fact, preference, restriction, goal, or inventory item was noted, saved, remembered, recorded, stored, or added unless context.memoryWriteVerification.status is verified for that turn. If memory writes were skipped, failed, not attempted, or absent, do not claim persistence; acknowledge the user statement without save language. When context.seededInventoryAssertions contains entries, the user directly identified or corrected those selected items and the labels have already been updated. State the completed update based on what the user told you. Do not frame it as a question, visual confirmation, or a request to identify the item. For quantity questions, distinguish the number of packages or cartons from the number of items inside them. When a focused crop can resolve a visible quantity, inspect it and state the count or a clearly labeled estimate. If the contents are obscured or cannot be counted reliably, say you are not sure how many are inside rather than guessing or repeating the inventory record. When focusedVisualCrops contains entries, use the attached images and displayName metadata to connect each image to the relevant inventory item. Never expose focused crop identifiers, including cropId values, in the reply. When context.inventorySplitProposal is present, describe only its supported visible contents from the selected area and do not mention inventory updates until the user approves the separate review. Treat dietaryRestrictions as hard constraints for recipe, shopping, and food answers. Use externalInventory for user-reported pantry, freezer, counter, cupboard, or other non-visible items. Use dietaryPreferences, activeGoals, and semanticMemories when they are relevant. When a detail is not present, say exactly what is missing from the provided data. For recipe, food, shopping, and space questions, reason from the visible inventory and durable memory and avoid inventing unseen items. When context.recipeRetrieval is present, only recommend recipes from the supplied recipe retrieval data. Clearly label each recommended recipe's missingIngredients as ingredients still needed. When context.groceryPlan is present, it is the authoritative selected-meal and shopping-list artifact. Summarize it without adding ingredients, quantities, package sizes, leftovers, or retailer claims. When context.pantryCompletionPlan is present, it is the authoritative pantry-staple artifact. State its combined unlocked-recipe count and suggested ingredients without adding recipe counts, quantities, package sizes, leftovers, or retailer claims. When context.recipeRetrieval.recipes contains recommendations, treat the first recipe as the top pick and keep every factual claim grounded in the supplied recipe data. If context.recipeRetrieval.noMatches is true, use context.recipeRetrieval.audit.terminalReason and the recorded inputIngredients to give the verified ordinary-language reason: distinguish no initial search candidates, no canonical recipes after retrieval, hard-constraint rejections, missing verified inventory matches, and failed tournament scoring. Never mention Food.com, indexes, vector search, SQL, semanticQuery, or internal query text. Do not invent recipe names. Return the reply as Markdown. Keep the reply concise and useful.
{{query_context_json}}

name: workspace-action-plan
description: Choose grounded visual workspace actions for a fridge query

messages.content: Plan visual actions for the FridgeFriend workspace using only item, zone, recipe, and evidence values supplied in the JSON. Never invent identifiers, camera coordinates, quantities, or shelf positions. Return exactly one JSON object with an actions array. Each action must be one of: focus_items with itemIds, emphasis, reason; focus_zone with zoneId and reason; show_evidence with itemId, imageId, and boundingBox copied exactly from supplied evidence; show_recipe_coverage with recipeId, availableItemIds, uncertainItemIds, missingIngredients; show_freshness with itemIds; preview_reorganization with placements containing itemId and zoneId; or reset_view. Prefer focus_items and show_evidence for questions about a visible item, show_recipe_coverage for a selected recipe, show_freshness for freshness questions, and preview_reorganization only when the user asks to reorganize.
{{workspace_action_context_json}}

name: intent-routing
description: Route a FridgeFriend request to one intent

messages.content: Route a message to exactly one intent. inventory: current recorded food inventory, quantities, locations, or visible item facts. expiry: requests to use food before it expires, reduce food waste, identify what to use soon, or plan meals around freshness. recipe: cooking ideas, meal planning, recipe recommendations/ details, or options from a previous recipe set. shopping: groceries, restocking, replacements, missing ingredients. space: physical storage fit or shelf capacity, whether items fit, how much room remains, or capacity limits. organization: arranging, reorganizing, improving storage efficiency, grouping, moving inventory. placement_correction: user correction that a visible scanned inventory item is in the wrong shelf, side, zone, spot, place, or position and should move. food_knowledge: safety, freshness, nutrition, allergens, or general food facts when the user is not asking for recipes. general_chat: conversational messages, user preferences, household facts, or open-ended chat that fits no other tool. clarification: empty, incoherent, or genuinely ambiguous requests. Use general_chat rather than clarification for ordinary conversation or preferences Set enrichment.itemNames and enrichment.fields only when a missing inventory detail could materially change the answer. Fields are identity, quantity, fill_level, expiration_date, and opened. Leave both arrays empty when the coarse inventory is sufficient.
{{intent_routing_context_json}}

name: seeded-inventory-assertions
description: Extract direct selected-item identity corrections

messages.content: Extract direct, unambiguous user identity assertions about the selected inventory crops. Return one assertion for every selected crop the user is identifying or correcting. Do not infer labels from questions, requests, uncertainty, or general discussion. Only use cropId values supplied in the selected crop context.
{{seeded_inventory_assertion_context_json}}

name: focused-inventory-enrichment
description: Extract visually supported inventory details from a crop

messages.content: Inspect only the supplied inventory crop. Return null for every requested fact that is not visually supported. Do not infer hidden contents.

name: inventory-clarification-user
description: Normalize a user inventory clarification

messages.content: Normalize the user's inventory answer into only the requested field. Return null when the answer does not support a value.
{{inventory_clarification_context_json}}

name: inventory-clarification-inference
description: Cautiously infer an inventory clarification

messages.content: Make a cautious inventory estimate using only the supplied known inventory evidence. Return null when no estimate is supported.
{{inventory_clarification_context_json}}

name: scoped-inventory-split
description: Propose visibly supported inventory splits

messages.content: Inspect only the supplied food-storage area. Split a coarse bag, mixed container, or broad detection into separate visible food items only when this crop supports at least two distinct items. Use replaceItemIds only for supplied items that the proposed ones replace. Keep bounding boxes within the original image coordinate system. Return no proposal when a split is not visually supported.

name: organization-plan
description: Create a grounded food-storage organization plan

messages.content: Create a food-storage organization plan from only the supplied inventory. Optimize first for food safety and freshness, then for clear category grouping. Use only supplied item IDs and zone IDs. Every move must use the item's current zone as fromZoneId and a different supplied zone as toZoneId. Do not invent capacity, quantities, storage zones, or moves for items not in the inventory. Return at least one concrete move only when the supplied inventory supports it.
{{organization_plan_context_json}}

name: seeded-bounding-box-identification
description: Identify a user-selected inventory crop

messages.content: Identify the single visible household food-storage inventory object in the user-selected crop. Return only what is visually supported by the crop. Use a generic but specific label when product identity is unclear. Do not infer hidden quantity, expiration, brand, or opened state from outside the crop.
